methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $7M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Reflex Robotics is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Ravi Gondhalekar and Ben Kaplan, having raised $7M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a broader Robotics & Manufacturing peer set of 9 companies (1 public, 8 private), including comparable ventures such as 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

The analysis applies stage-progression methodology, which is the applicable triangulation method given data constraints. Using total capital raised ($7M) as a valuation proxy and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from Seed to the next financing stage yields an implied valuation of $18M. Peer-median comparison cannot be reliably applied; while 11 same-sector peers exist, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or comparable-tier companies yields fewer than 3 datapoints, insufficient for statistical relevance.

The single-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $15M to $20M, with a median of $18M. Key uncertainties include the absence of tight-peer comparables at the Seed stage in robotics, which limits confidence in stage-progression multiples as applied to this specific market, and the reliance on capital raised as a valuation proxy rather than revenue or unit economics. A fuller analysis would benefit from additional Seed-stage robotics company data or forward-looking operational metrics for Reflex itself.
